name: Alex Santos
description: marine bio M.S. student in Tacoma 🌊 intertidal inverts, low tides, the occasional sunset. mostly here to watch.

name: Kelp Forest Lab
description: Research lab studying kelp forest and nearshore ecology in the Salish Sea. Tweets by grad students. RTs ≠ endorsements.

name: Salish Sea Stories
description: Science storytelling from the inland sea. Orcas, bull kelp, herring, and the people who study them.

name: Mara Quigley
description: Intertidal photographer. I get up at 4am so you can see chitons in good light. Olympic Peninsula, WA.

name: Dr. Lena Carvalho
description: Marine ecologist. Sea star wasting disease + recovery. Professor somewhere rainy. She/her.

name: PNW Tide Watch
description: Daily tide windows, king tide alerts, and minus-tide forecasts for Washington and Oregon coasts.

name: SeaStar Watch
description: Community science program tracking sea star wasting and recovery from Alaska to California. Join a survey!

name: Nudibranch Daily
description: One absurdly beautiful sea slug per day. That's it. That's the account.

name: Cascadia Weather Watch
description: Forecast threads for western WA and OR. Atmospheric rivers, heat domes, and the eternal drizzle.

name: Tacoma Food Map
description: Eating my way through Grit City one taco truck at a time. Tips welcome.

name: Salish Sea Research Cooperative
description: Collaborative monitoring and research for a healthier Salish Sea. Reports, data, and field updates.

name: Moss & Madrone
description: Slow nature writing from the Pacific Northwest. Essays, field notes, and an unreasonable number of lichen photos.

name: Dr. Owen Fricke
description: Invertebrate zoologist. Chitons deserve better PR. Museum collections enjoyer.

name: Sasha Brandt
description: field tech. professional rock flipper (i put them back). my waders leak.

name: Jess Okada
description: Naturalist + tide pool walk leader. Ask me about anemone clone wars. WA coast, mostly.

name: Denny Walsh
description: Paddling every inlet of the south Sound eventually. Bioluminescence chaser.

name: Green Crab Watch WA
description: Tracking the European green crab invasion in Washington waters. Report sightings, save an estuary.

name: Olympic Coast Walks
description: Trail notes, tide warnings, and beach camping beta for the wild Olympic coast.

name: Grad School Gull
description: Screaming about academia so you don't have to. Memes, deadlines, despair, repeat.

name: Whole Watershed
description: Stream-to-sound conservation nonprofit. Salmon recovery, riparian restoration, volunteer days.

name: Theo Park
description: Former marine bio undergrad, current coffee person. I still know my barnacles.
